original: Brightest known object in the universe identified as a quasar, 500 trillion x brighter than the sun, powered by a supermassive black hole that devours a sun-sized mass every day
Four-year old Charlotte May Pierstorff mailed by train from Grangeville, Idaho to her grandparents’ house 73 miles away in most famous 'child in the post' instance
980 Japanese soldiers reportedly killed by crocodiles in two days on Ramree Island, Burma
NASA's Mars Odyssey space probe begins to map the surface of Mars using its thermal emission imaging system
USSR launches Mir space station into Earth orbit
Mickey Mouse welcomed in China
Jennifer Doudna, American biochemist (2020 Nobel Prize for Chemistry for CRISPR Gene Editing), born in Washington D.C.
Homer Hickam, American author (Rocket Boys) and retired NASA engineer, born in Coalwood, West Virgina
1st prize inserted into a Cracker Jack box

Thomas Edisonis granted a patent for his cylinder phonograph
Peruvian stratovolcano Huaynaputina explodes in the most violent eruption in South American recorded history
Space shuttle Enterprise makes 1st Test flight atop a 747 jetliner

Will Keith Kelloggjoins Charles D. Bolin in founding the Battle Creek Toasted Corn Flake Company, now the multinational food manufacturer Kellogg's, after falling out with his brother over development credit and wanting to add sugar to cereal
Children's educational TV program "Mister Rogers' Neighborhood" debuts on NET (now PBS)
UK flies ½ ton of Beatles wigs to the US
Bill Bowerman, American track and field coach and co-founder of Nike, born in Portland, Oregon (d. 1999)
1st brother combo to win Gold & Silver in same event at Olympics (Phil & Steve Mahre-Slalom)

American defending championDick Buttonbecomes 1st figure-skater to land a triple jump in competition; performs triple loop in Olympic free skate in Oslo; wins gold medal ahead of Austria’s Helmut Seibt
Canadian team of Justin Kripps & Alexander Kopacz dead-heat in 3:16.86 with Germans Francesco Friedrich & Thorsten Margis for the 2-man bobsleigh gold medal at the Pyeongchang Winter Olympics
William Schroeder is 1st artificial heart patient to leave hospital He spends 15 minutes outside Humana Hospital in Louisville, Kentucky

Paul McCartney, his girlfriend Jane Asher, andRingo Starrand his wife Maureen travel to India, joiningJohn Lennon,George Harrisonand their wives to study Transcendental Meditation for two months withMaharishi Mahesh Yogiat his ashram in Rishikesh; Ringo only stays for 2 weeks
